expectations.text: model_and_parts_created: Script creates a model with at least two parts: a cam (fixed to ground) and a follower
expectations.evidence: part create rigid_body for .model.cam and .model.follower both present; cam fixed to ground via fixed joint

expectations.text: translational_joint: A translational joint constrains the follower to translate along X
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint translational for .model.follower_trans using markers oriented with zâ†’global +X (orientation = 90D, 90D, 0D)

expectations.text: uses_step5_not_step: STEP5 is used for the cam force (not plain STEP)
expectations.evidence: force function uses STEP5(DX(...),0.0,0.0,20.0,500.0) â€” no plain STEP(...) anywhere

expectations.text: correct_dx_range_0_to_20: STEP5 x-range spans 0 to 20 (follower displacement 0 to 20 mm)
expectations.evidence: STEP5(DX(.model.follower.tip_mkr,.model.cam.ref_mkr),0.0,0.0,20.0,500.0) â€” x0=0.0, x1=20.0

expectations.text: correct_force_range_0_to_500: STEP5 output range spans 0 to 500 (force 0 to 500 N)
hold_beyond_20mm: Force holds at 500 N for DX > 20 mm (STEP5 naturally saturates at h1)
expectations.evidence: STEP5 saturates at h1=500.0 for DX > 20.0 mm by definition; no clamp needed

expectations.text: velocity_damping_2_vx: Damping term 2.0 * VX(...) added to force expression
expectations.evidence: Second part of function expression: " - 2.0*VX(.model.follower.tip_mkr,.model.cam.ref_mkr)"

expectations.text: quoted_expression: Full FUNCTION= expression wrapped in double quotes
expectations.evidence: Both parts of the expression are in double quotes using Adams line continuation: "STEP5(...)" , & " - 2.0*VX(...)"; Adams ran it successfully

expectations.text: geometry_present: At least one geometry shape added to each part (cam and follower)
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape cylinder for .model.cam.cam_cyl (r=40, l=30) and .model.follower.follower_cyl (r=10, l=60)

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All markers, parts, joints and forces use fully qualified names e.g. .model.cam.ref_mkr, .model.follower_trans

expectations.text: no_adams_id: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'adams_id' in cam_follower.cmd returned no matches

expectations.text: simulate_transient: Script uses simulation single_run transient command
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient with end_time=1.0, number_of_steps=1000, model_name=.model

expectations.text: adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: run_adams_cmd.py exited with code 0; the two grep hits for 'ERROR:' in aview.log are inside '!' comment lines (TypeError and SyntaxError from Python traceback text), not Adams ERROR: lines

expectations.text: uses_impact: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: Response uses IMPACT(...) explicitly in both the standalone expression and the CMD snippet.

expectations.text: correct_displacement_arg: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
expectations.evidence: First argument is DZ(.model.part.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref), measuring Z-height of CM above the floor reference.

expectations.text: correct_velocity_arg: Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
expectations.evidence: Second argument is VZ(.model.part.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref), the time derivative of the DZ measure.

expectations.text: threshold_at_zero: Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
expectations.evidence: Third argument (trigger) is 0.0, contact activates when DZ < 0.0.

expectations.text: correct_params: Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present
expectations.evidence: K=1.0E5, e=1.5, C=50.0, d=0.1 â€” all four values present and correct in the IMPACT() call.

expectations.text: quoted_expression: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: In the CMD snippet, function = starts with a double-quoted string; long expression is split across three double-quoted continuation lines concatenated by Adams.

expectations.text: fixes_model_create_abbreviation: 'model cre' expanded to 'model create'
expectations.evidence: File contains 'model create model_name = pendulum'; comment on line 3 confirms 'model cre -> model create'

expectations.text: fixes_part_create_abbreviation: 'part cre rig nam' expanded to 'part create rigid_body name_and_position'
expectations.evidence: File contains 'part create rigid_body name_and_position &'; comment on line 4 confirms fix from 'part cre rig nam'

expectations.text: changes_mass_properties_create_to_modify: 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' changed to 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties'
expectations.evidence: File uses 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties &' (not 'part create'); comment on line 5 confirms the fix

expectations.text: removes_adams_id: 'adams_id = 3' removed
expectations.evidence: No occurrence of 'adams_id' anywhere in the fixed script; comment on line 6 confirms removal

expectations.text: removes_center_of_mass_marker: 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm' is NOT present in the final script
expectations.evidence: 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm' is present in part modify rigid_body mass_properties block; model also explicitly creates .pendulum.link.cm marker â€” both are prohibited by the rubric

expectations.text: fixes_missing_dot_paths: 'pendulum.link.pin_mkr' corrected to '.pendulum.link.pin_mkr' (missing leading dot fixed in both marker create and i_marker_name)
expectations.evidence: marker_name = .pendulum.link.pin_mkr (leading dot present) and i_marker_name = .pendulum.link.pin_mkr (leading dot present); comment line 8-9 confirms both fixes

expectations.text: fixes_spring_damper_keyword: 'force create element_like spring_damper' changed to 'force create element_like translational_spring_damper' and 'length = 200.0' changed to 'displacement_at_preload = 200.0'
expectations.evidence: File uses 'force create element_like translational_spring_damper' and 'displacement_at_preload = 200.0'; comments on lines 10-11 confirm both fixes

expectations.text: fixes_simulation_command: 'sim trans end_time = 2.0 step_size = 0.001' changed to 'simulation single_run transient' with proper parameters
expectations.evidence: File uses 'simulation single_run transient &' with type, end_time, number_of_steps, model_name, initial_static parameters; comment line 12 confirms fix

expectations.evidence: No 'ERROR:' lines in aview.log; simulation completed ('End Simulation', 'batch run is finished', 'Finished -----'); run_adams_cmd.py exited with code 0

expectations.text: explains_when_to_use: Explains that spring_damper is for 1-DOF along a line of action
